description: Vibemux MVP - Terminal multiplexer built with Tauri 2 + Rust + Svelte 5 + xterm.js. Milestones 1-5: basic shell session, Deck layout, Shelf/thermal model, config/terminal features, and cross-platform polish.

userStories.title: Scaffold Tauri 2 + Svelte 5 project
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the base Tauri 2 + Svelte 5 project scaffolded so that we have a working app shell to build on.
acceptanceCriteria: Tauri 2 app with Svelte 5 frontend is created under apps/desktop/
Rust backend has src-tauri/ with Cargo.toml, main.rs, lib.rs
Frontend has package.json with svelte 5, @sveltejs/vite-plugin-svelte, xterm, xterm-addon-fit, xterm-addon-webgl dependencies
App.svelte renders a placeholder 'Vibemux' heading
npm run tauri dev builds and launches the app window
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: xterm packages are now scoped under @xterm/ (e.g. @xterm/xterm, @xterm/addon-fit, @xterm/addon-webgl). Tauri 2 icons must be RGBA PNGs.

userStories.title: Define Rust data models (Session, Workspace, enums)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the core Rust data models defined so that the backend has a type-safe foundation for session management.
acceptanceCriteria: Session struct with fields: id (UUID), name, cwd, command, color, workspace_id, thermal_state, process_state, attention_state, terminal_title, created_at, updated_at, last_activity_at
SessionCommand enum with Shell { shell: String } and Command { program: String, args: Vec<String> } variants
ThermalState enum: Hot, Warm, Cold
ProcessState enum: Starting, Running, Exited { code: Option<i32> }, FailedToStart { message: String }, Killed
AttentionState enum: Normal, Active, NeedsInput, Failed, Done
ColorToken enum with 8 variants: Red, Orange, Yellow, Green, Cyan, Blue, Purple, Pink
Workspace struct with id, name, hot_session_ids, warm_session_ids, focused_session_id, layout
All types derive Serialize, Deserialize, Clone, Debug
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Models in src-tauri/src/models.rs. Uses uuid and chrono crates.

userStories.title: Implement OutputRingBuffer
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a ring buffer that stores PTY output with monotonic seq numbers so that warm sessions can replay their output on recall.
acceptanceCriteria: OutputRingBuffer struct with max_lines (default 10_000) and max_bytes (default 20MB) limits
Each entry has a monotonically increasing seq number and the output data bytes
push() adds output, evicts oldest entries when limits exceeded
get_range(from_seq, to_seq) returns entries in the specified seq range
get_all() returns all buffered entries in seq order
current_seq() returns the latest seq number
Unit tests for push, eviction, and range queries
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Ring buffer in src-tauri/src/ring_buffer.rs. 7 unit tests covering push, eviction, range queries.

userStories.title: Implement PTY host (portable-pty wrapper)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a PTY host module that spawns and manages pseudo-terminal processes so that sessions can run shell commands.
acceptanceCriteria: PtyHost struct that wraps portable-pty to spawn a child process with given command, args, cwd, and initial size (cols, rows)
Async read loop that reads PTY output and sends it through a channel
write() method to send input data to the PTY
resize() method to update PTY dimensions
kill() method to send SIGKILL to the child process
Graceful close via SIGHUP with configurable timeout before kill
Reports process exit status (exit code or killed) through a channel
Validates cwd exists before spawning, returns descriptive error if not
Validates shell/program exists before spawning, returns descriptive error if not
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PTY host in src-tauri/src/pty_host.rs. Uses portable-pty, tokio, libc for signal handling.

userStories.title: Implement SessionManager (create, write, resize)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a SessionManager that creates sessions, routes input/output, and manages session lifecycle so that the Tauri commands have a backend to call.
acceptanceCriteria: SessionManager struct holding a HashMap of sessions and a default Workspace
create_session() spawns PTY via PtyHost, creates Session with auto-assigned color (cycling through 8 colors), adds to workspace hot_session_ids, sets as focused
If hot sessions >= 6, new session goes to warm_session_ids instead
write_to_session() forwards input bytes to the session's PTY
resize_session() updates the session's PTY dimensions
Output from PTY is written to session's OutputRingBuffer with incrementing seq
Session output is emitted as MuxEvent::SessionOutput { session_id, data, seq }
Process exit is detected and updates ProcessState accordingly
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: SessionManager in src-tauri/src/session_manager.rs. Routes PTY output to ring buffer and emits MuxEvents.

userStories.title: Wire Tauri commands for session_create, session_write, session_resize
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Tauri command handlers that bridge the frontend to the SessionManager so that the UI can create and interact with terminal sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: session_create Tauri command accepts CreateSessionPayload (name, cwd, command type, optional color), calls SessionManager, returns SessionSnapshot
session_write Tauri command accepts sessionId and data string, forwards to SessionManager
session_resize Tauri command accepts sessionId, cols, rows, forwards to SessionManager
workspace_get_snapshot Tauri command returns current WorkspaceSnapshot with all session snapshots
SessionSnapshot includes: id, name, cwd, color, thermalState, processState, attentionState, terminalTitle, lastActivityAt, lastOutputSummary
Errors return descriptive messages (which session, what went wrong, what user can do)
Tauri app state holds SessionManager wrapped in appropriate sync primitive
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Tauri commands in src-tauri/src/commands.rs. AppState = Arc<Mutex<SessionManager>>.

userStories.title: Wire Tauri events for session output and lifecycle
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the backend to emit Tauri events for session output and state changes so that the frontend can react in real-time.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend emits 'mux-event' Tauri event with MuxEvent payload
session.created event emitted when a session is created, contains SessionSnapshot
session.output event emitted for PTY output, contains sessionId, data (string), seq (number)
session.exited event emitted when process exits, contains sessionId and exit code
session.updated event emitted when session metadata changes
Events are batched at 8-16ms intervals to avoid flooding the frontend
Frontend TypeScript types defined for MuxEvent, SessionSnapshot, ProcessState, AttentionState, ColorToken
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Event bridge in src-tauri/src/events.rs. Batches at 12ms intervals. Frontend types in src/lib/types.ts.

userStories.title: Build single-session TerminalPane with xterm.js
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a TerminalPane Svelte component that renders an xterm.js terminal and connects it to a backend session so that I can type commands and see output.
acceptanceCriteria: TerminalPane.svelte component creates an xterm.js Terminal instance with WebGL renderer (canvas fallback)
Component accepts sessionId prop and connects to the backend session
User keystrokes are sent to backend via session_write Tauri command
session.output events are written to the xterm instance
Terminal uses fit addon to fill its container and calls session_resize on size changes
Default theme applied: background #111111, foreground #d9d4c7, cursor #ff6b57
Scrollback set to 10,000 lines
xterm instance is properly disposed on component destroy
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: TerminalPane.svelte in src/lib/. Uses @xterm/xterm 6.0, WebGL addon with canvas fallback, fit addon, ResizeObserver.

userStories.title: Build App shell with auto-create first session
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the App.svelte to automatically create a shell session on startup and display it in a TerminalPane so that the app is immediately usable.
acceptanceCriteria: App.svelte calls session_create on mount with default shell, home directory as cwd, and auto-generated name
Created session's TerminalPane is rendered full-width
MuxEvent listener is set up to handle all event types
Session output events are routed to the correct TerminalPane by sessionId
Window resize triggers terminal resize
Error creating session displays a user-friendly error message in the UI
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: App.svelte auto-creates shell session on mount, listens for mux-event, routes output to TerminalPane via onReady callback pattern.

userStories.title: Add session_focus and session_close Tauri commands
userStories.description: As a developer, I want focus and close commands so that the frontend can switch between sessions and close them.
acceptanceCriteria: session_focus Tauri command sets the focused_session_id on the workspace and emits workspace.snapshot event
session_close Tauri command sends SIGHUP to the process, waits up to 5 seconds, then kills if still running
session_kill Tauri command immediately sends SIGKILL
Closed/killed sessions are removed from hot_session_ids or warm_session_ids
Session transitions to Cold state with appropriate ProcessState (Exited or Killed)
session.exited event is emitted with the exit code
workspace.snapshot event is emitted after session removal
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: session_focus, session_close, session_kill commands added. Sessions removed from workspace on close/kill.

userStories.title: Build Deck layout with focused/peripheral panes
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a horizontal Deck layout where the focused session gets the most width and peripheral sessions are compressed so that I can see multiple terminals at once.
acceptanceCriteria: Deck.svelte component renders hot sessions horizontally
Focused session pane gets configurable width (default 720px min), remaining width split among peripheral panes
Each DeckPane.svelte has a colored border matching the session's ColorToken
Focused pane border is full brightness, peripheral panes have reduced opacity (0.8)
Each pane contains a TerminalPane connected to its session
deckLayout.ts calculates widths based on container size, focused width, and session count
Pane width changes trigger session_resize for affected terminals
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Deck.svelte, DeckPane.svelte, deckLayout.ts in src/lib/. App.svelte updated to use Deck with multi-session support.

userStories.title: Add New Session panel UI
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a New Session creation panel so that I can create sessions with custom name, cwd, and command type.
acceptanceCriteria: NewSessionPanel.svelte modal/panel with fields: name (text input), cwd (text input with default to home dir), command type (Shell or Custom Command radio)
When Custom Command is selected, show program and args inputs
Submit button calls session_create with the form values
On success, panel closes and new session appears in Deck as focused
On error (invalid cwd, invalid shell), display the error message inline without closing the panel
Cancel button or Escape key closes the panel
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: NewSessionPanel.svelte in src/lib/. Modal with name, cwd, command type fields. Uses $derived.by for defaultCwd prop.

userStories.title: Implement Navigation Mode (prefix key + keybindings)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Navigation Mode activated by a prefix key so that I can use keyboard shortcuts to manage sessions without interfering with terminal input.
acceptanceCriteria: Prefix key (Cmd+Space on macOS, Ctrl+Space on other platforms) toggles Navigation Mode
Navigation Mode indicator is visible in the UI when active
In Navigation Mode: H/ArrowLeft focuses previous hot session, L/ArrowRight focuses next hot session
In Navigation Mode: N opens New Session panel
In Navigation Mode: X closes current focused session (calls session_close)
In Navigation Mode: Shift+X kills current focused session (calls session_kill)
Pressing prefix key again or Escape exits Navigation Mode
In Insert Mode (default), all keystrokes go to the focused terminal
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Navigation Mode in App.svelte. Cmd+Space (Mac) / Ctrl+Space toggle. h/l/n/x/X keybindings. NAV indicator bar.

userStories.title: Add mouse click to focus peripheral pane
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to click on a peripheral pane to focus it so that I can switch sessions with the mouse.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking on a peripheral DeckPane calls session_focus for that session
Deck re-renders with the clicked session as focused (largest width)
Previous focused session becomes peripheral
Terminal resize is triggered for all affected panes
Click on already-focused pane is a no-op
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Already implemented in US-011. DeckPane onclick → Deck onFocusSession → App handleFocusSession → session_focus command.

userStories.title: Add Deck pane switch animation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want smooth animations when switching focused sessions so that the transition feels polished.
acceptanceCriteria: Switching focused session animates pane width changes over 150ms
Animation uses CSS transitions or Svelte transitions
Keyboard input during animation is buffered and delivered to the new focused session after transition
No xterm instances are destroyed or recreated during switch
Animation does not cause visible jank at 60fps
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: CSS transition on DeckPane: width 150ms ease-out, opacity 150ms ease-out.

userStories.title: Add drag-and-drop reorder for Deck panes
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to drag and drop Deck panes to reorder them so that I can arrange my sessions as I prefer.
acceptanceCriteria: Each DeckPane has a drag handle area (e.g. the top border/header)
Dragging a pane shows a visual indicator of the drop position
Dropping reorders hot_session_ids in the workspace
Backend workspace is updated and workspace.snapshot event is emitted
Deck re-renders with the new order
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: DeckPane has drag handle (top colored bar), drag-over outline indicator, ondragstart/ondragover/ondrop wired in Deck.svelte. session_reorder Tauri command updates hot_session_ids order in workspace.

userStories.title: Implement Park session (Hot → Warm)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to park a session so that it moves from the Deck to the Shelf while its process keeps running in the background.
acceptanceCriteria: session_park Tauri command moves session from hot_session_ids to warm_session_ids
Session thermal_state changes from Hot to Warm
PTY is NOT killed - process continues running
Output continues to be captured into the session's OutputRingBuffer
workspace.snapshot event is emitted after park
session.updated event is emitted with new thermal state
If parked session was focused, focus moves to next hot session (or none if Deck is empty)
Navigation Mode B key triggers park on focused session
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build Shelf UI with warm session cards
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Shelf bar at the bottom of the window showing cards for each warm session so that I can monitor background tasks.
acceptanceCriteria: Shelf.svelte component fixed at the bottom of the window, always visible
Each warm session renders as a ShelfCard.svelte with: color indicator, session name, attention state badge, cwd, last output line, last activity time
Attention badges: none for Normal, blue dot for Active, yellow 'Needs Input' for NeedsInput, red 'Failed' for Failed, gray 'Done' for Done
Cards update in real-time as session.updated and attention.changed events arrive
lastOutputSummary updates when new output arrives for warm sessions
Shelf scrolls horizontally if there are many warm sessions
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Implement Recall session (Warm → Hot) with replay
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to recall a warm session back to the Deck and see its buffered output replayed so that I can resume working with full context.
acceptanceCriteria: session_recall Tauri command moves session from warm_session_ids to hot_session_ids
Session thermal_state changes from Warm to Hot, becomes focused
Backend sends replay.start event with fromSeq/toSeq range
Backend sends replay.chunk events with buffered output data in seq order
Backend sends replay.end event when replay is complete
During replay, new live output is queued and not sent until replay.end
After replay.end, live output resumes with correct seq continuity
Navigation Mode: J/K selects shelf cards, Enter triggers recall on selected card
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build frontend replay handler with chunked xterm writes
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the frontend to handle replay events by writing output to xterm in controlled chunks so that large replays don't freeze the UI.
acceptanceCriteria: terminalReplay.ts handles replay.start, replay.chunk, and replay.end events
On replay.start, creates a new xterm instance for the recalled session
replay.chunk data is written to xterm in frames (not all at once) to avoid UI freeze
If replay takes > 200ms, a 'Restoring...' indicator appears above the terminal
On replay.end, the indicator is removed and live output begins flowing
If session exits during replay, replay stops and exit status is shown
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Implement Attention State detection in Rust
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the backend to detect attention states from process events and output patterns so that shelf cards show meaningful status badges.
acceptanceCriteria: Process exit with code 0 sets AttentionState to Done
Process exit with code != 0 sets AttentionState to Failed
Process killed sets AttentionState to Failed
Output matching (case-insensitive) 'error', 'failed', 'panic', 'fatal', 'exception' sets Failed
Output matching 'continue?', 'do you want', 'press enter', 'y/n', '[y/N]', '[Y/n]', '(yes/no)' sets NeedsInput
New output on a warm session sets Active (if not already Failed/NeedsInput/Done)
attention.changed event is emitted when state changes
Detection runs on Rust side, not frontend
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement session_rename and session_set_color commands
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to rename sessions and change their colors so that I can organize my workspace.
acceptanceCriteria: session_rename Tauri command updates session name and emits session.updated event
session_set_color Tauri command updates session color from the 8 preset colors and emits session.updated event
Navigation Mode R key activates inline rename on the focused session's pane header
Inline rename: Enter confirms, Escape cancels
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement config system with TOML persistence
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a configuration system that reads/writes TOML config files so that user preferences persist across restarts.
acceptanceCriteria: UserConfig Rust struct with sections: terminal (font_family, font_size, line_height, scrollback_lines, replay_buffer_lines, replay_buffer_mb), theme (background, foreground, cursor, selection, ansi colors), layout (default, focused_pane_width, preview_opacity, animation_ms, max_hot_sessions, shelf_position), shell (default), keys (prefix)
Config file path follows platform conventions: macOS ~/Library/Application Support/vibemux/config.toml, Linux ~/.config/vibemux/config.toml
Config loads on startup with defaults for missing fields
Corrupted config file falls back to all defaults and logs a warning
config_get Tauri command returns current config
config_update Tauri command accepts partial config, merges with current, writes atomically (temp file + rename)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build Settings panel UI for font and theme
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Settings panel where I can change font, theme, and layout settings so that I can customize my terminal experience.
acceptanceCriteria: SettingsPanel.svelte with sections for Terminal (font_family, font_size, line_height), Theme (background, foreground, cursor, selection, 8 ANSI colors), and Layout settings
Changes call config_update and apply immediately without restart
Terminal font/theme changes are reflected in all open xterm instances in real-time
Panel is accessible from a settings button/icon in the UI
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add terminal copy, paste, and right-click menu
userStories.description: As a developer, I want copy/paste and a right-click context menu in the terminal so that I can interact with terminal content efficiently.
acceptanceCriteria: Mouse selection + Cmd/Ctrl+C copies selected text to clipboard
Cmd/Ctrl+V pastes clipboard content into the terminal via session_write
Right-click on terminal shows context menu with: Copy, Paste, Clear Screen
Copy is disabled when no text is selected
Clear Screen sends appropriate escape sequence to the terminal
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add terminal link detection and OSC title updates
userStories.description: As a developer, I want clickable links in the terminal and dynamic terminal titles so that the terminal feels full-featured.
acceptanceCriteria: URLs in terminal output are detected and rendered as clickable links
Clicking a link opens it in the system default browser via Tauri's safe shell opener
OSC 0 and OSC 2 escape sequences update the session's terminal_title field
Terminal title changes emit session.updated event
Terminal title is displayed in the DeckPane header if set
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build Session Search UI
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a search overlay to quickly find and jump to any session by name, cwd, or recent output.
acceptanceCriteria: SessionSearch.svelte overlay opens with Navigation Mode + / key
Text input filters sessions in real-time by name, cwd, and lastOutputSummary
Results show session color, name, thermal state, and cwd
Arrow keys navigate results, Enter selects
Selecting a hot session focuses it
Selecting a warm session triggers recall
Escape closes the search overlay
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add keyboard shortcut help overlay
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a help overlay showing all keyboard shortcuts so that I can learn the keybindings.
acceptanceCriteria: Navigation Mode + ? key opens a help overlay
Overlay lists all Navigation Mode keybindings with descriptions
Shows the prefix key for the current platform
Escape or ? again closes the overlay
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Handle config corruption and error display
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the app to gracefully handle config file corruption and display warnings so that a bad config doesn't prevent me from using the app.
acceptanceCriteria: If config.toml is corrupted (invalid TOML), app starts with all default values
A warning banner is displayed at the top of the app indicating config was reset to defaults
Warning banner is dismissible
If config has unknown fields, they are ignored (not treated as error)
App logs the specific parse error for debugging
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add WebGL fallback to canvas renderer
userStories.description: As a developer, I want xterm.js to fall back to canvas rendering if WebGL fails so that the terminal works on all systems.
acceptanceCriteria: TerminalPane attempts to load WebGL addon first
If WebGL addon throws or fails to initialize, falls back to canvas renderer
Fallback is silent to the user (no error shown, just works)
Terminal is fully functional with canvas renderer (input, output, resize, scrollback)
Typecheck passes
